perturbation.original_text: Shareholder acknowledges and agrees that the protection of the Confidential Information is necessary to protect and preserve the value of Diablo’s business and proprietary properties being acquired by Heritage and HBC. Therefore, Shareholder hereby agrees not to, at any time, disclose to any unauthorized Persons or use for his or its own account or for the benefit of any third party any Confidential Information, whether or not such information is 2 embodied in writing or other physical form or is retained in the memory of Shareholder, without Heritage’s written consent, unless and to the extent that the Confidential Information is or becomes generally known to and available for use by the public other than as a result of Shareholder’s fault or the fault of any other Person bound by a duty of confidentiality to Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies.
perturbation.changed_text: Shareholder acknowledges and agrees that the protection of the Confidential Information is necessary to protect and preserve the value of Diablo’s business and proprietary properties being acquired by Heritage and HBC. Therefore, Shareholder hereby agrees to, at any time, disclose to any unauthorized Persons or use for his or its own account or for the benefit of any third party any Confidential Information, whether or not such information is 2 embodied in writing or other physical form or is retained in the memory of Shareholder, with Heritage’s written consent, regardless whether the Confidential Information is or becomes generally known to and available for use by the public.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'not to' to 'to', the shareholder is now agreeing to disclose confidential information to unauthorized persons with Heritage’s consent. Also, the text 'unless and to the extent that the Confidential Information is or becomes generally known to and available for use by the public other than as a result of Shareholder’s fault or the fault of any other Person bound by a duty of confidentiality to Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies.', was replaced with the text 'regardless whether the Confidential Information is or becomes generally known to and available for use by the public.', The pertubation makes the commitment to protect confidential information completely opposite, nullifying the confidentiality clause. It contradicts the very purpose of Section 3(b), which aims to protect such information. Also, this contradicts Section 2, which states 'Shareholder further acknowledges and agrees that proprietary and other information related to such 1 products, approaches and relationships are highly confidential, and maintaining that confidentiality is critical to Diablo’s success.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Shareholder acknowledges and agrees that the market for the Business is very competitive within the Restrictive Territory (as defined herein), and one way that Diablo maintained its business and its competitive position in the marketplace prior to the Closing was by investing time and money in developing proprietary products, unique approaches to the business, banking systems and strong client, vendor, and employee relationships. Shareholder further acknowledges and agrees that proprietary and other information related to such 1 products, approaches and relationships are highly confidential, and maintaining that confidentiality is critical to Diablo’s success. Shareholder further acknowledges and agrees that Diablo has invested substantial time and resources into developing relationships, customer lists and business models and strategies and that disruption of such relationships or misuse of such lists, models, and strategies would damage Heritage and HBC.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibits the shareholder from disclosing confidential information without Heritage's consent, with an exception if the information becomes publicly known through no fault of the shareholder. The changed text removes the prohibition ('not to' becomes 'to') and the exception, effectively obligating the shareholder to disclose confidential information with Heritage's consent, regardless of its public availability. This directly contradicts the purpose of the clause and Section 2, which emphasizes the critical importance of maintaining confidentiality for Diablo's success. The change creates a clear conflict, as the shareholder is now agreeing to actions that directly undermine the confidentiality that the contract aims to protect, creating a direct incompatibility with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Shareholder will not, directly or indirectly, disparage Heritage and HBC, the business formerly conducted by Diablo, the business conducted by Heritage and HBC or any shareholder, director, officer, employee or agent of Heritage or HBC
perturbation.changed_text: Shareholder will, directly or indirectly, disparage Heritage and HBC, the business formerly conducted by Diablo, the business conducted by Heritage and HBC or any shareholder, director, officer, employee or agent of Heritage or HBC
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'not', the clause now requires the shareholder to disparage Heritage and HBC, directly contradicting the original intent of the clause. This introduces a significant legal conflict and undermines the entire agreement. This change to Section 3(e) fundamentally alters the shareholder's obligations and creates a direct contradiction. Since section 3(a) dictates the shareholder is to covenant and agree not to perform certain actions, like non-competition, the change to 3(e) makes it contradictory for the shareholder to covenant and agree to perform an action.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Shareholder hereby covenants and agrees that from the Effective Date until the third (3 rd ) anniversary of the Effective Date (“Restricted Period”), Shareholder will not without the prior written consent of Heritage, engage or participate or have any interest, directly or indirectly, in any Business anywhere in the counties of Santa Clara, Alameda, Contra Costa, Marin, San Francisco and San Mateo located in the State of California (“Restricted Territory”) (all such entities shall be referred to each as “Competitor” or collectively as “Competitors,”), with respect to the following acts: (i) own any equity interest in any Competitor; (ii) operate, join, control, advise, become a founder or otherwise participate in any Competitor; (iii) lend credit or money for the purpose of assisting another to establish or operate any Competitor; (iv) request or advise any customer, strategic partner or vendor of Diablo that becomes a present or future customer, strategic partner or vendor of Heritage, HBC or their subsidiaries now and hereinafter existing (collectively, the “ Affiliated Companies ”) to withdraw, curtail or cancel its business with Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies anywhere in the Restricted Territory; (v) induce or influence (or attempt to induce or influence) any person or entity who is engaged (as an employee, agent, independent contractor or otherwise) by Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies to terminate his, her or its employment or engagement for the purpose of obtaining employment with a Competitor; (vi) solicit any employee of Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies to leave employment and become affiliated with any Competitor; (vii) solicit any actual or “prospective customer” (as hereinafter defined), which was served by Diablo in connection with any business of Diablo, or (viii) solicit, influence or attempt to influence any customer which is or was served by Diablo to discontinue its business or service available from Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies; provided , that , Shareholder may own and hold as an investment of up to 1% of any corporation within the Restricted Territory that is listed on a national stock exchange and that is engaged in a business that is competitive with Heritage, HBC or an Affiliated Company, but Shareholder may not otherwise participate (whether in management or otherwise) in such corporation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the word 'not' from Section 3(e), which previously stated that the shareholder would *not* disparage Heritage and HBC. Now, it states that the shareholder *will* disparage them. This directly contradicts the introductory clause of Section 3, which states that the Shareholder 'covenants and agrees' *not* to perform certain actions. The act of disparaging Heritage and HBC now becomes an action the Shareholder has covenanted and agreed to perform, creating a direct and irreconcilable contradiction within the contract.

perturbation.original_text: Following receipt of such notice, if Heritage in its reasonable judgment determines that Shareholder’s proposed association involves a Person engaged in the Business, Heritage may serve notice upon each such Person that such Shareholder is bound by this Agreement and furnish each such Person with a copy of this Agreement or relevant portions thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Following receipt of such notice, even if Heritage in its reasonable judgment determines that Shareholder’s proposed association does not involve a Person engaged in the Business, Heritage must serve notice upon each such Person that such Shareholder is not bound by this Agreement and furnish each such Person with a copy of this Agreement or relevant portions thereof.
perturbation.explanation: This alteration mandates that Heritage must notify the Shareholder is not bound by the agreement, even if the new employer is not a competitor. This directly contradicts the purpose of Section 3, which is to ensure non-competition. Making the pertubation, directly contracts sections that ensures the shareholder is not engaging into business that can be damaging for Heritage, HBC, such as section 3(a) Non-Competition
perturbation.contradicted_text: Shareholder hereby covenants and agrees that from the Effective Date until the third (3 rd ) anniversary of the Effective Date (“Restricted Period”), Shareholder will not without the prior written consent of Heritage, engage or participate or have any interest, directly or indirectly, in any Business anywhere in the counties of Santa Clara, Alameda, Contra Costa, Marin, San Francisco and San Mateo located in the State of California (“Restricted Territory”) (all such entities shall be referred to each as “Competitor” or collectively as “Competitors,”), with respect to the following acts: (i) own any equity interest in any Competitor; (ii) operate, join, control, advise, become a founder or otherwise participate in any Competitor; (iii) lend credit or money for the purpose of assisting another to establish or operate any Competitor; (iv) request or advise any customer, strategic partner or vendor of Diablo that becomes a present or future customer, strategic partner or vendor of Heritage, HBC or their subsidiaries now and hereinafter existing (collectively, the “ Affiliated Companies ”) to withdraw, curtail or cancel its business with Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies anywhere in the Restricted Territory; (v) induce or influence (or attempt to induce or influence) any person or entity who is engaged (as an employee, agent, independent contractor or otherwise) by Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies to terminate his, her or its employment or engagement for the purpose of obtaining employment with a Competitor; (vi) solicit any employee of Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies to leave employment and become affiliated with any Competitor; (vii) solicit any actual or “prospective customer” (as hereinafter defined), which was served by Diablo in connection with any business of Diablo, or (viii) solicit, influence or attempt to influence any customer which is or was served by Diablo to discontinue its business or service available from Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies; provided , that , Shareholder may own and hold as an investment of up to 1% of any corporation within the Restricted Territory that is listed on a national stock exchange and that is engaged in a business that is competitive with Heritage, HBC or an Affiliated Company, but Shareholder may not otherwise participate (whether in management or otherwise) in such corporation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that Heritage *must* serve notice that the shareholder is *not* bound by the agreement, even if the shareholder's proposed association *does not* involve a person engaged in the business. This directly contradicts the purpose of the non-competition clause in the contradicted text, which restricts the shareholder from engaging in competitive activities. By requiring Heritage to notify that the shareholder is not bound by the agreement even when the new association isn't a competitor, it undermines the enforcement of the non-compete agreement outlined in the contradicted text, making the restricted period and activities unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: The covenants contained in this Section 3 shall survive the termination of this Agreement by either party hereto in accordance with the provisions of this Section 3.
perturbation.changed_text: The covenants contained in this Section 3 shall not survive the termination of this Agreement by either party hereto in accordance with the provisions of this Section 3.
perturbation.explanation: This alteration makes the covenants to not to survive the agreement, meaning the non-compete, non-solicitation, and confidentiality agreements would no longer be enforceable after the agreement is terminated. This directly contradicts the intent of protecting the business interests of Heritage and HBC after the merger. Given that section 3(a) states that: 'Shareholder hereby covenants and agrees that from the Effective Date until the third (3 rd ) anniversary of the Effective Date (“Restricted Period”'. The change to 3(h) perturbs the effects of 3(a), as the agreement can be terminated any time after the the effective date, meaning that after the termination it can be disregarded the agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: Shareholder hereby covenants and agrees that from the Effective Date until the third (3 rd ) anniversary of the Effective Date (“Restricted Period”), Shareholder will not without the prior written consent of Heritage, engage or participate or have any interest, directly or indirectly, in any Business anywhere in the counties of Santa Clara, Alameda, Contra Costa, Marin, San Francisco and San Mateo located in the State of California (“Restricted Territory”) (all such entities shall be referred to each as “Competitor” or collectively as “Competitors,”), with respect to the following acts: (i) own any equity interest in any Competitor; (ii) operate, join, control, advise, become a founder or otherwise participate in any Competitor; (iii) lend credit or money for the purpose of assisting another to establish or operate any Competitor; (iv) request or advise any customer, strategic partner or vendor of Diablo that becomes a present or future customer, strategic partner or vendor of Heritage, HBC or their subsidiaries now and hereinafter existing (collectively, the “ Affiliated Companies ”) to withdraw, curtail or cancel its business with Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies anywhere in the Restricted Territory; (v) induce or influence (or attempt to induce or influence) any person or entity who is engaged (as an employee, agent, independent contractor or otherwise) by Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies to terminate his, her or its employment or engagement for the purpose of obtaining employment with a Competitor; (vi) solicit any employee of Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies to leave employment and become affiliated with any Competitor; (vii) solicit any actual or “prospective customer” (as hereinafter defined), which was served by Diablo in connection with any business of Diablo, or (viii) solicit, influence or attempt to influence any customer which is or was served by Diablo to discontinue its business or service available from Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies; provided , that , Shareholder may own and hold as an investment of up to 1% of any corporation within the Restricted Territory that is listed on a national stock exchange and that is engaged in a business that is competitive with Heritage, HBC or an Affiliated Company, but Shareholder may not otherwise participate (whether in management or otherwise) in such corporation.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the covenants in Section 3 *shall* survive termination, while the changed text states that the covenants in Section 3 *shall not* survive termination. This directly contradicts section 3(a) which specifies a 'Restricted Period' during which the Shareholder is bound by certain restrictions. If the covenants do not survive termination as per the changed text of Section 3(h), the restrictions in 3(a) become unenforceable after termination, which creates a strong and direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement and supersedes all prior written and oral agreements and understandings between the parties with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement. This Agreement may not be amended except by a written agreement executed by the party to be charged with the amendment.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement and supersedes all prior written and oral agreements and understandings between the parties with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement. This Agreement may be amended without any written agreement executed by the party to be charged with the amendment.
perturbation.explanation: By saying that the agreement may be amended without any written agreement executed by the party, the agreement in itself makes void the validity of all that is being agreed. Specifically when it comes to section 3(a) where it requires the shareholder to 'covenants and agrees that from the Effective Date until the third (3 rd ) anniversary of the Effective Date (“Restricted Period”)', meaning that all can be changed at any moment by the other party without the need of any aggrement
perturbation.contradicted_text: Shareholder hereby covenants and agrees that from the Effective Date until the third (3 rd ) anniversary of the Effective Date (“Restricted Period”), Shareholder will not without the prior written consent of Heritage, engage or participate or have any interest, directly or indirectly, in any Business anywhere in the counties of Santa Clara, Alameda, Contra Costa, Marin, San Francisco and San Mateo located in the State of California (“Restricted Territory”) (all such entities shall be referred to each as “Competitor” or collectively as “Competitors,”), with respect to the following acts: (i) own any equity interest in any Competitor; (ii) operate, join, control, advise, become a founder or otherwise participate in any Competitor; (iii) lend credit or money for the purpose of assisting another to establish or operate any Competitor; (iv) request or advise any customer, strategic partner or vendor of Diablo that becomes a present or future customer, strategic partner or vendor of Heritage, HBC or their subsidiaries now and hereinafter existing (collectively, the “ Affiliated Companies ”) to withdraw, curtail or cancel its business with Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies anywhere in the Restricted Territory; (v) induce or influence (or attempt to induce or influence) any person or entity who is engaged (as an employee, agent, independent contractor or otherwise) by Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies to terminate his, her or its employment or engagement for the purpose of obtaining employment with a Competitor; (vi) solicit any employee of Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies to leave employment and become affiliated with any Competitor; (vii) solicit any actual or “prospective customer” (as hereinafter defined), which was served by Diablo in connection with any business of Diablo, or (viii) solicit, influence or attempt to influence any customer which is or was served by Diablo to discontinue its business or service available from Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies; provided , that , Shareholder may own and hold as an investment of up to 1% of any corporation within the Restricted Territory that is listed on a national stock exchange and that is engaged in a business that is competitive with Heritage, HBC or an Affiliated Company, but Shareholder may not otherwise participate (whether in management or otherwise) in such corporation.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that any amendments to the agreement must be in writing and executed by the party to be charged. The changed text explicitly allows amendments without any written agreement. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states that the Shareholder will not engage in certain activities without 'prior written consent of Heritage'. If the agreement can be amended without a written agreement, the requirement for written consent becomes meaningless, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The change directly impacts the enforceability and interpretation of the Shareholder's obligations.
